preamble.summary: Enoch sees the storehouses of winds, the cornerstone of the earth, and the pillars of heaven. He observes seven stars bound together like great mountains, burning in fire — fallen stars punished for failing to rise at their appointed times. An angel explains that this is the prison of rebellious stars/angels.
preamble.remarkable: The identification of stars with angels — and the punishment of stars that fail to keep their courses — directly parallels the Watchers' own punishment. In ancient cosmology, stars were often identified with heavenly beings (cf. Job 38:7, 'the morning stars sang together').
preamble.friction: The blending of astronomical phenomena with angelic beings may seem strange to modern readers but is standard in ancient Near Eastern and Israelite thought.
preamble.connections: Job 38:7 (morning stars as heavenly beings); Isaiah 14:12 (fallen star/morning star); Jude 13 (wandering stars for whom blackest darkness is reserved); Revelation 1:20 (stars as angels of churches).

verses.text_reference: I saw the treasuries of all the winds: I saw how He had furnished with them the whole creation and the firm foundations of the earth.
verses.rendering: I saw the storehouses of all the winds. I saw how God had furnished the whole creation with them, and the firm foundations of the earth.

verses.text_reference: And I saw the corner-stone of the earth: I saw the four winds which bear [the earth and] the firmament of the heaven.
verses.rendering: I saw the cornerstone of the earth, and I saw the four winds that support the earth and the vault of heaven.
translator_notes: The 'cornerstone of the earth' echoes Job 38:6 ('on what were its bases sunk, or who laid its cornerstone?'). The four winds supporting creation reflect the fourfold cosmic architecture common in ancient Near Eastern thought.

verses.text_reference: And I saw how the winds stretch out the vaults of heaven, and have their station between heaven and earth: these are the pillars of the heaven.
verses.rendering: I saw how the winds stretch out the vault of heaven and hold their position between heaven and earth — these are the pillars of heaven.
translator_notes: Winds as pillars of heaven — a cosmological concept where atmospheric forces serve a structural role in maintaining the cosmic architecture.

verses.text_reference: I saw the winds of heaven which turn and bring the circumference of the sun and all the stars to their setting.
verses.rendering: I saw the winds of heaven that turn and carry the sun's circuit and all the stars to their setting.

verses.text_reference: I saw the winds on the earth carrying the clouds: I saw the paths of the angels. I saw at the end of the earth the firmament of the heaven above.
verses.rendering: I saw the winds on the earth carrying the clouds. I saw the paths of the angels. At the end of the earth I saw the vault of heaven above.

verses.text_reference: And I proceeded and saw a place which burns day and night, where there are seven mountains of magnificent stones, three towards the east, and three towards the south.
verses.rendering: I continued onward and saw a place that burns day and night, where there are seven mountains of magnificent stones — three toward the east and three toward the south.

verses.text_reference: And as for those towards the east, one was of coloured stone, and one of pearl, and one of jacinth, and those towards the south of red stone.
verses.rendering: Those toward the east: one was of colored stone, one of pearl, and one of jacinth. Those toward the south were of red stone.
translator_notes: The precious stone mountains anticipate the jeweled foundations of the New Jerusalem (Revelation 21:19-20). The correspondence may not be coincidental — both describe the cosmic sacred space.

verses.text_reference: But the middle one reached to heaven like the throne of God, of alabaster, and the summit of the throne was of sapphire.
verses.rendering: But the middle one reached to heaven like the throne of God — made of alabaster — and the summit of the throne was sapphire.
translator_notes: The sapphire throne summit echoes Exodus 24:10 ('under his feet as it were a pavement of sapphire stone') and Ezekiel 1:26 ('above the likeness of a throne was something that looked like sapphire'). Sapphire is consistently associated with the divine throne.

verses.text_reference: And I saw a flaming fire. And beyond these mountains
verses.rendering: I saw a blazing fire. And beyond those mountains —

verses.text_reference: is a region the end of the great earth: there the heavens were completed.
verses.rendering: there is a region at the end of the great earth, where the heavens come to their completion.
translator_notes: 'Where the heavens were completed' — the meeting point of heaven and earth at the cosmic boundary, a concept found in Mesopotamian and Israelite cosmology.

verses.text_reference: And I saw a deep abyss, with columns of heavenly fire, and among them I saw columns of fire fall, which were beyond measure alike towards the height and towards the depth.
verses.rendering: I saw a deep abyss with columns of heavenly fire, and among them I saw columns of fire falling, immeasurable in both height and depth.

verses.text_reference: And beyond that abyss I saw a place which had no firmament of the heaven above, and no firmly founded earth beneath it: there was no water upon it, and no birds, but it was a waste and horrible place.
verses.rendering: Beyond that abyss I saw a place that had no vault of heaven above it and no solid earth beneath it. There was no water on it and no birds — it was a desolate and terrifying place.
translator_notes: A place with no heaven, no earth, no water, no life — this is the anti-creation, the undoing of Genesis 1. It is where the structures of created order cease to exist.

verses.text_reference: I saw there seven stars like great burning mountains, and to me, when I inquired regarding them,
verses.rendering: There I saw seven stars like great burning mountains. When I asked about them,

verses.text_reference: The angel said: 'This place is the end of heaven and earth: this has become a prison for the stars and the host of heaven.
verses.rendering: the angel said: 'This place is the end of heaven and earth. It has become a prison for the stars and the host of heaven.
translator_notes: Stars imprisoned for disobedience — the same judgment that befell the Watchers. In the ancient worldview, stars were animate heavenly beings (Job 38:7), and their 'wandering' from appointed courses was rebellion. This directly informs Jude 13 ('wandering stars, for whom the gloom of utter darkness has been reserved forever').

verses.text_reference: And the stars which roll over the fire are they which have transgressed the commandment of the Lord in the beginning of their rising, because they did not come forth at their appointed times.
verses.rendering: The stars that roll through the fire are those that transgressed the Lord's commandment at the beginning of their rising, because they did not come forth at their appointed times.
translator_notes: Stars punished for not rising on schedule — cosmic disobedience at the most basic level. The regularity of creation that chapters 2-5 celebrated is here shown to have exceptions, which are immediately punished.

verses.text_reference: And He was wroth with them, and bound them till the time when their guilt should be consummated (even) for ten thousand years.'
verses.rendering: And he was angry with them and bound them until the time when their guilt will be fully punished — for ten thousand years.'
translator_notes: Ten thousand years as a punishment period reflects the apocalyptic tendency toward vast temporal scales, distinguishing cosmic punishment from ordinary human timelines.
